description_long: This is a multi-story brick apartment building with a steep, dark gray gabled roof featuring two chimneys and several dormer windows. The front facade is symmetrical, with three floors of large, rectangular windows framed by stone trim and a prominent central entrance with a decorative stone arch. The side walls are plain brick with minimal window openings, while the rear facade has a simpler arrangement of windows and no visible entrance. The roof includes both dormer and skylight windows, adding architectural interest and natural light to the upper floor. The building's exterior is primarily composed of reddish-brown brickwork, accented by gray stone and slate roofing. Its overall appearance suggests a traditional European architectural style, likely from the late 19th or early 20th century.
description: A traditional multi-story brick apartment building with a steep gabled roof, symmetrical window arrangement, and a prominent central entrance framed by stone. The exterior features reddish-brown brick, gray stone trim, and multiple dormer and skylight windows.
description_view_0: The front view shows a symmetrical facade with three floors of large windows, a central wooden door with a decorative stone arch, and three dormer windows on the steep roof.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain brick wall with a single skylight on the roof and no windows or doors on the wall itself.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a simpler arrangement with smaller windows, no entrance, and two skylights on the roof.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a plain brick wall with a single skylight and no additional features.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on the front facade, with evenly spaced groupings on each floor and aligned horizontally and vertically. The side and rear facades have fewer, irregularly spaced windows.
